motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: 16°F, clear and brittle. No wind. The lake surface is flat enough to read from.

undercurrent.note: The second grey envelope is the escalation beat: a recipient name (R. Calloway, rhyming with E. Calloway already in the stack), a declarative statement about the dock, and a first signature — H. H is not Harrow's first name; it is the correspondent's initial, and it is also the first assertion of authorial identity. The town's calibration of Gerald's new schedule (Marigold adjusting the coffee start time) is the town's accommodation in its most domestic form. The fifteen-and-a-half inch threshold Gerald names aloud is the first time he has framed a measurement as a threshold rather than a number — a shift from record-keeping to interpretation. Twenty-two-foot clarity and the shape of the Ames rowboat is Toby's next partial description of the lakebed — an object, not a feature, not yet named directly. Box now holds nine items, which is the census number, which is not an accident but has not been named yet. Next beat: the box reaches ten items (one over census), or a stranger's car is seen on Route 9, or Russ asks someone other than Wren about the letters.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
